# forgekit runtime install-unit — dry-run evidence (lane B / axis 4)
# Generated by running the real CLI; nothing was installed or executed.
# Templates are read from the package source (templates_root); the demo
# --repo-root below is just substituted into the rendered unit. forgekit_bin
# shows 'forgekit' here (no installed entrypoint on PATH in this sandbox);
# a real host resolves it via shutil.which('forgekit').

## launchd (macOS)
$ forgekit runtime install-unit --launchd --dry-run --repo-root /Users/you/local-dev/yule-studio-agent --interval 300

# backend: launchd
# target:  /Users/masterway/Library/LaunchAgents/com.forgekit.runtime.plist
# --- rendered unit ---
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<!--
  ForgeKit control-plane daemon LaunchAgent (GW7).

  Runs `forgekit runtime serve` as a per-USER LaunchAgent on a Mac mini host
  (docs/control-plane-architecture.md §3: Mac mini = 1차 ForgeKit host). This is
  the bounded always-on path — the daemon's own loop is bounded (heartbeat /
  kill-switch / cooldown); KeepAlive restarts it if it exits.

  Reproducible config: the daemon reads the SAME ~/.forgekit/config.json as the
  Linux/systemd path — only the supervisor (launchd vs systemd) differs. Do NOT
  bake machine-specific hacks here.

  Placeholders to substitute before loading (see README.md):
    forgekit   absolute path to the installed `forgekit` entrypoint
                       (e.g. /Users/<you>/.venvs/forgekit/bin/forgekit)
    /Users/you/local-dev/yule-studio-agent      absolute path to the checkout the daemon operates on
    /Users/masterway/.forgekit  data home (default /Users/<you>/.forgekit)
    /Users/masterway      /Users/<you>
    300       serve poll interval in seconds (default 300)

  HONEST LIMIT (macOS): a closed lid suspends the host — a LaunchAgent does NOT
  keep running through clamshell sleep. For true 24h, run lid-open with
  `caffeinate -s` (or `sudo pmset -c sleep 0`), or use the Linux/systemd path
  (the 1급 always-on host). This file does not pretend otherwise.
-->
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>Label</key>
    <string>com.forgekit.runtime</string>

    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    <array>
        <string>forgekit</string>
        <string>runtime</string>
        <string>serve</string>
        <string>--interval</string>
        <string>300</string>
        <string>--repo-root</string>
        <string>/Users/you/local-dev/yule-studio-agent</string>
    </array>

    <key>EnvironmentVariables</key>
    <dict>
        <key>FORGEKIT_HOME</key>
        <string>/Users/masterway/.forgekit</string>
    </dict>

    <key>WorkingDirectory</key>
    <string>/Users/you/local-dev/yule-studio-agent</string>

    <!-- Start at login and keep alive (the serve loop is itself bounded). -->
    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
    <true/>
    <key>KeepAlive</key>
    <dict>
        <key>SuccessfulExit</key>
        <false/>
    </dict>

    <!-- Don't hammer-restart a misconfigured daemon. -->
    <key>ThrottleInterval</key>
    <integer>30</integer>

    <!-- Logs under the user's Library/Logs (NOT the repo — no runtime state in git). -->
    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
    <string>/Users/masterway/Library/Logs/forgekit/runtime.out.log</string>
    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
    <string>/Users/masterway/Library/Logs/forgekit/runtime.err.log</string>

    <key>ProcessType</key>
    <string>Background</string>
</dict>
</plist>
# --- install commands ---
mkdir -p /Users/masterway/Library/LaunchAgents
mkdir -p /Users/masterway/Library/Logs/forgekit
write -> /Users/masterway/Library/LaunchAgents/com.forgekit.runtime.plist
launchctl bootout gui/501 /Users/masterway/Library/LaunchAgents/com.forgekit.runtime.plist
launchctl bootstrap gui/501 /Users/masterway/Library/LaunchAgents/com.forgekit.runtime.plist
# --- restart (after `git pull` + reinstall) ---
launchctl kickstart -k gui/501/com.forgekit.runtime
# DRY-RUN: nothing written, no launchctl/systemctl executed.

## systemd (Linux)
$ forgekit runtime install-unit --systemd --dry-run --repo-root /opt/yule-studio-agent --interval 300

# backend: systemd
# target:  /Users/masterway/.config/systemd/user/forgekit-runtime.service
# --- rendered unit ---
[Unit]
# ForgeKit control-plane daemon (Linux/systemd 1급 always-on path).
#
# Counterpart of deploy/launchd/com.forgekit.runtime.plist — runs the SAME
# `forgekit runtime serve` loop and reads the SAME ~/.forgekit/config.json. Only
# the supervisor (systemd vs launchd) differs; do NOT bake machine-specific
# hacks here. The serve loop is itself bounded (heartbeat / kill-switch /
# cooldown); Restart=on-failure brings it back if it crashes.
#
# Installed as a per-USER unit (systemctl --user) so it needs no root and keeps
# logs / state in the user's scope. For a headless host that must run without an
# active login session, enable lingering once: `loginctl enable-linger <user>`.
#
# Placeholders substituted by `forgekit runtime install-unit --systemd`
# (see README.md for the manual sed fallback):
#   forgekit   absolute path to the installed `forgekit` entrypoint
#   /opt/yule-studio-agent      absolute path to the checkout the daemon operates on
#   /Users/masterway/.forgekit  data home (default ~/.forgekit)
#   300       serve poll interval in seconds
Description=ForgeKit control-plane runtime daemon
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
WorkingDirectory=/opt/yule-studio-agent
Environment=FORGEKIT_HOME=/Users/masterway/.forgekit
ExecStart=forgekit runtime serve --interval 300 --repo-root /opt/yule-studio-agent
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=30s
# Don't hammer-restart a misconfigured daemon.
StartLimitIntervalSec=300
StartLimitBurst=5
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal
# forgekit runtime serve installs its own kill-switch handling; give the
# in-flight tick time to finish on SIGTERM.
KillSignal=SIGTERM
TimeoutStopSec=30s

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
# --- install commands ---
mkdir -p /Users/masterway/.config/systemd/user
write -> /Users/masterway/.config/systemd/user/forgekit-runtime.service
systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user enable --now forgekit-runtime.service
# --- restart (after `git pull` + reinstall) ---
systemctl --user restart forgekit-runtime.service
# DRY-RUN: nothing written, no launchctl/systemctl executed.
